query: I'm about to present to the board on our platform strategy next Tuesday. Can you put together a visualisation of our value chain showing what's custom-built vs commodity? We're a B2B legal-tech SaaS.

ok so I need to understand where we should be investing our engineering budget in 2026. We're a logistics startup, roughly Series B. Help me think through what layers of our stack are commoditized vs where we should double down.
map our competitive landscape — we run an e-scooter rental service in Berlin, competing with Lime and Voi. Where are the evolution frontiers?
I keep hearing about Wardley mapping from my CTO. Can you walk me through what ours would look like for our accounts-payable automation product?
strategic positioning exercise for our roadmap. our core thing is property management software for landlords. what moves should we be considering given the market?
I need to figure out build vs buy for our identity verification component. We're a fintech onboarding SMBs. give me the wider context across all our components too.
help me visualise the value chain of our grocery delivery service — from the user need through to the dark store infra.
We just closed Series A. Need to articulate to the board where our strategic moat is. Can you help me do a proper landscape analysis of our med-tech diagnostics platform?
put a wardley map together for a neobank targeting gig workers — clients are the riders and the bank is licensed in the UK
help me think through where each part of our value chain sits on the genesis-commodity spectrum — we sell observability tooling to DevOps teams at mid-market SaaS co's.
Can you draw me a flowchart of the user journey for our signup flow? First screen is email, then OTP, then a payment page.
Help me write a positioning statement for our product — Geoffrey Moore style. For [target customer] who [pain] our product is a [category] that [benefit].
I need a data pipeline diagram showing how events flow from our Kafka topics through to Snowflake. Mermaid output please.
Can you build me a skills matrix of my team — rows are people, columns are competencies, each cell is a level rating 1-5. I'll share the list in a moment.
Show me a MECE breakdown of the reasons SMB customers churn from our accounting SaaS in the first 90 days.
I need a swimlane diagram of our order-to-cash process with the responsible team per step — Sales, CS, Finance, Ops.
Can you help me think through porter's five forces for our regional cloud hosting market? We're based in Brazil.
make a grid of build/buy decisions for the components I send you — just a two-column markdown table, no coordinates or visualisation.
quick question — what's the difference between a Wardley map and a value stream map? We keep getting them mixed up.
I have this really messy architecture diagram from our consultants. Can you clean it up and make it render properly in Excalidraw?
